57: <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, I hear you were thrust into the pages of an enchanted book! Forgive me if I say I'm more than a little envious.

58: I've heard of <Emphasis>The Merchant's Tale</Emphasis> and its popularity within Corvos before. Still, I never knew about this most singular edition of the story...

59: Y'rhul Nunh's reputation was well deserved. I've worked in the same field as him for many years, but never come close to making such a momentous discovery.

60: While inside the book, I take it you saw the Corvos of old? From a time before imperial rule?

61: Verdant plains of green that shone like emeralds... Even if the story may have embellished some aspects, I can scarcely imagine how striking it must have been!

62: Corvos is known for its natural beauty, but the idyllic land must have been transformed into a paradise by the storyteller's imagination.

63: The beautiful scenery made breathtaking, and the dangerous beasts made deadly. Oh, if only I could peruse the pages myself!

64: My only recourse is to build upon the work of the past and make the tale even more fantastic! I consider it a challenge to my own imagination!

65: Besides, I have the benefit of countless generations of history and information to draw upon. Surely I can add elements which will bring even more thrills to readers.

66: Picture this, if you will: in the storybook setting of Corvos, a young merchant sets off in search of the one treasure which will bring joy to his beloved. However, standing between him and his goal is a veritable army of mythic beasts!

67: Yet his stalwart companion is there to face down each and every one! Weapons clash, blood spills, and spirits soar! With all the passion of the original authors, I shall describe battles of sheer intensity.

68: <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, you must promise to read it when I'm done, if only to experience the world of <Emphasis>The Merchant's Tale</Emphasis> in a new light!
